decision_summary: The design is feasible with a chip max temperature of 58.6°C, well below the 85°C constraint. To further minimize the objective, I will increase the heat spreader conductivity. This is the highest priority lever for improving lateral heat spreading and reducing peak temperature.

changes.reason: No change applied: The spreader conductivity is already at its upper bound (500.0 W/(m*K)). Further improvement via this variable is impossible.

expected_effects: No immediate effect from this specific change as the limit is reached.
Future iterations should focus on geometric changes (increasing spreader width or height) to continue lowering the temperature.
risk_notes: Since the primary material lever is saturated, the next steps must involve geometry modifications which may have tighter constraints or manufacturing implications.
Diminishing returns may be approached as the system nears the thermal resistance limit of the base plate and boundary conditions.
